message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
Read https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker and Create `Dockerfile.unsloth` in `.devcontainer`. Follow `uv` installation as in other Dockerfile.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- git-commit: Commit code changes to git with careful review and detailed commit messages. Use when Claude needs to stage and commit code changes to a git repository. This skill ensures changes are reviewed with git diff before staging, excludes markdown files (except README.md), and generates comprehensive commit messages following conventional commit standards.
- code-implementation: Implement code changes with a structured workflow. Use when Claude needs to implement features, fix bugs, or make code changes in a repository. Includes repository exploration, branch creation, implementation planning, unit testing, coverage checks, documentation, and git commits. Ensures quality through testing and proper documentation.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
FROM nvidia/cuda:13.0.0-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.content:
1	# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
2	#
3	# Why a separate image:
4	# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
5	# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
6	# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
7	# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
8	# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
9	# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
10	# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
11	#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
12	#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
13	# fired on every linear layer in the model.
14	#
15	# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
16	# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
17	# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
18	#
19	# Usage:
20	#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
21	#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
22	#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
23	#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.
24	
25	FROM nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04
26	
27	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
28	
29	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
30	    git \
31	    curl \
32	    ca-certificates \
33	    python3 \
34	    python3-pip \
35	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
36	
37	COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/
38	
39	ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy
40

file.content:
# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
#
# Why a separate image:
# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
# fired on every linear layer in the model.
#
# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.

FROM nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.content:
1	FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:1-3.12-bookworm
2	
3	# Remove stale Yarn apt repo (ships with base image, has expired GPG key)
4	RUN rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list
5	
6	# Update package lists and install dependencies
7	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
8	    wget \
9	    curl \
10	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
11	
12	# Upgrade pip and install uv
13	RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install uv
14	
15	# Install Go
16	RUN wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
17	    && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
18	    && rm go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz
19	ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
20	ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin:$GOPATH/bin
21	
22	# Install Go tools (protobuf, coverage merge, linting)
23	RUN go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest \
24	    && go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest \
25	    && go install github.com/wadey/gocovmerge@latest \
26	    && go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest \
27	    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/go
28	
29	# Install protoc (protobuf compiler)
30	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends protobuf-compiler \
31	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
32	
33	# Install Node.js using NodeSource repository (more reliable than NVM in Docker)
34	RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | bash - \
35	    && apt-get install -y nodejs
36	
37	# Verify installations
38	RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version
39

file.content:
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:1-3.12-bookworm

# Remove stale Yarn apt repo (ships with base image, has expired GPG key)
RUN rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list

# Update package lists and install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Upgrade pip and install uv
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install uv

# Install Go
RUN wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && rm go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz
ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin:$GOPATH/bin

# Install Go tools (protobuf, coverage merge, linting)
RUN go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest \
    && go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest \
    && go install github.com/wadey/gocovmerge@latest \
    && go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest \
    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/go

# Install protoc (protobuf compiler)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends protobuf-compiler \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js using NodeSource repository (more reliable than NVM in Docker)
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | bash - \
    && apt-get install -y nodejs

# Verify installations
RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version

aiTitle: Create Dockerfile.unsloth for devcontainer with uv

content.content:
# Unsloth Docker Setup Summary

Based on the documentation provided, I cannot extract a full Dockerfile because **the actual Dockerfile contents are not included in this page**. The documentation references a pre-built image rather than providing build instructions.

## What's Actually Available

The page documents how to **use** the existing Docker image, not how to build it:

**Image name**: `unsloth/unsloth` (available on Docker Hub)

**Quick start command**:
```bash
docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
  -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
  -v $(pwd)/work:/workspace/work \
  --gpus all \
  unsloth/unsloth
```

## Key Configuration Details

The documentation specifies these environment variables:
- `JUPYTER_PASSWORD` (default: `unsloth`)
- `JUPYTER_PORT` (default: `8888`)
- `SSH_KEY` (optional)
- `USER_PASSWORD` (default: `unsloth`)

**Container structure**: `/workspace/work/`, `/workspace/unsloth-notebooks/`, `/home/unsloth/`

## To Access Full Build Details

Since the Dockerfile itself isn't documented here, you could consult the actual Docker Hub repository at `unsloth/unsloth` or use the query mechanism to ask for Dockerfile specifications directly.

content.text: The Unsloth docs reference a prebuilt image `unsloth/unsloth` rather than providing a Dockerfile. I'll base `Dockerfile.unsloth` on that image and add `uv` following the same pattern as `Dockerfile`/`Dockerfile.cuda128`.

input.content:
# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
#
# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
#
# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
# Dockerfile.cuda128).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth

FROM unsloth/unsloth

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

toolUseResult.content:
# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
#
# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
#
# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
# Dockerfile.cuda128).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth

FROM unsloth/unsloth

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.text: Created `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth`. It uses the official `unsloth/unsloth` prebuilt image as the base (per the Unsloth docs, which only document image usage rather than a buildable Dockerfile), then adds `uv` via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest` and sets `UV_LINK_MODE=copy` — same pattern as `Dockerfile` and `Dockerfile.cuda128`.
